specs_source: https://www.royalcaribbeanpresscenter.com/ (official ship Fact Sheets served from royalcaribbeanmedia.com)

ships.name: Icon of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage January 27, 2024. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The first ship in Royal Caribbean's revolutionary Icon Class, sailing since January 2024 with eight distinct neighborhoods, seven pools and Category 6 — the largest waterpark at sea.

activities.name: Category 6
activities.desc: The largest waterpark at sea, with six record-breaking waterslides.

activities.name: Frightening Bolt
activities.desc: Waterslide billed as the tallest slide at sea.

activities.name: Pressure Drop
activities.desc: The first open free-fall slide at sea.

activities.name: Storm Surge & Hurricane Hunter
activities.desc: Family raft and mat-racing slides within Category 6.

activities.name: Crown's Edge
activities.desc: Part skywalk, part ropes course and part thrill ride, suspended over the ocean off the side of the ship.

activities.name: Sports Court
activities.desc: Open court for basketball and sports.

activities.name: Royal Bay Pool
activities.desc: The largest pool at sea, holding over 40,000 gallons, in the Chill Island neighborhood.

activities.name: The Hideaway
activities.desc: The first suspended infinity pool at sea, in the adults-only Hideaway neighborhood.

activities.name: Ice Skating
activities.desc: Open ice sessions at the Absolute Zero ice arena.

exp.who_for: Multi-generational families and thrill-seekers above all: Surfside is the line's first neighborhood built end-to-end for young families, while Category 6's six record-breaking slides, Crown's Edge and Adrenaline Peak target adrenaline lovers, and adults get the suspended, adults-only Hideaway pool.

dining.name: Main Dining Room
dining.desc: Complimentary multi-course dinner across a three-deck dining room.

dining.name: AquaDome Market
dining.desc: Royal Caribbean's first food hall, with five made-to-order stands under the AquaDome.

dining.name: Surfside Eatery
dining.desc: Complimentary family buffet in the Surfside neighborhood.

dining.name: Surfside Bites
dining.desc: Grab-and-go quick bites for families in Surfside.

dining.name: Basecamp
dining.desc: Complimentary casual eatery in Thrill Island.

dining.name: Park Cafe
dining.desc: Bagels, made-to-order salads and classics for breakfast and lunch in Central Park, opening at night for tapas.

dining.name: Empire Supper Club
dining.desc: Swanky eight-course tasting experience with live jazz.

dining.name: The Mason Jar Southern Restaurant & Bar
dining.desc: Southern comfort food and Southern-style brunch.

dining.name: Izumi in the Park
dining.desc: All-day walk-up window for fresh sushi in Central Park.

dining.name: Giovanni's Italian Kitchen & Wine Bar
dining.desc: Italian dining and wine bar on the Royal Promenade.

dining.name: Playmakers Sports Bar & Arcade
dining.desc: Sports bar with pub fare and an arcade.

entertainment.name: The Wizard of Oz
entertainment.desc: Full Broadway musical production — Royal Caribbean's biggest cast and orchestra ever — with a new 16-piece orchestra, characters soaring above the audience and 600+ costume elements.

entertainment.name: Aqua Action!
entertainment.desc: High-diving AquaTheater production in the AquaDome with Olympic-level divers, skateboarders, aerialists, slackliners and synchronized swimmers, plus a double 3D flying system and diving-board robots.

entertainment.name: Absolute Zero
entertainment.desc: Royal Caribbean's biggest ice arena ever, reengineered for bigger skating feats.

entertainment.name: Starburst: Elemental Beauty
entertainment.desc: Ice show at Absolute Zero interpreting the periodic table of the elements.

entertainment.name: Once Upon a Time: The King's Royal Ball
entertainment.desc: Family-friendly ice show where fairy-tale icons revisit revamped storylines.

entertainment.name: Showband! Live. Music. Now.
entertainment.desc: Live music showcase.

entertainment.name: The Effectors
entertainment.desc: Original stage production.

entertainment.name: Lou's Jazz 'n Blues
entertainment.desc: Live jazz and blues venue in Central Park.

entertainment.name: Dueling Pianos
entertainment.desc: Interactive sing-along piano bar.

entertainment.name: Music Hall
entertainment.desc: Multi-level live music and nightlife venue.

entertainment.name: Spotlight Karaoke
entertainment.desc: Karaoke bar with private and main rooms.

neighbourhoods.name: Thrill Island
neighbourhoods.desc: Adventure neighborhood home to Category 6, the largest waterpark at sea, plus Crown's Edge, FlowRider and Lost Dunes mini-golf.

neighbourhoods.name: Chill Island
neighbourhoods.desc: Multi-deck pool neighborhood with several of the ship's seven pools, including Royal Bay, the largest pool at sea.

neighbourhoods.name: Surfside
neighbourhoods.desc: The first Royal Caribbean neighborhood designed end-to-end for young families, with a pool, carousel and family dining.

neighbourhoods.name: Central Park
neighbourhoods.desc: Open-air neighborhood with more than 33,500 real plants, live jazz at Lou's Jazz 'n Blues and al-fresco dining.

neighbourhoods.name: The Hideaway
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only retreat centered on the first suspended infinity pool at sea.

neighbourhoods.name: Royal Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: The social heart of the ship with more than 15 restaurants, bars and lounges.

neighbourhoods.name: Suite Neighborhood
neighbourhoods.desc: Exclusive multi-deck enclave for suite guests, including The Grove suite sun deck and pool.

neighbourhoods.name: AquaDome
neighbourhoods.desc: Crown of the ship topped by the largest single piece of glass and steel Royal Caribbean has put to sea, home to the AquaDome Market food hall and AquaTheater performances.

kids_family.name: Surfside
kids_family.desc: Whole neighborhood built for young families, with pool, carousel, eateries and play spaces.

kids_family.name: Splashaway Bay
kids_family.desc: Aquapark play zone for kids with slides and sprayers.

kids_family.name: Adventure Ocean
kids_family.desc: Kids club with interactive games and programming by age group.

kids_family.name: Social020
kids_family.desc: Teens-only hangout with gaming, music and events.

exp.deploy_note: Caribbean & The Bahamas, ex-Miami (PortMiami), year-round; alternating 7-night Eastern & Western Caribbean, every sailing calling at Perfect Day at CocoCay.

ships.name: Star of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage August 31, 2025. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The second Icon Class ship, which debuted from Port Canaveral in August 2025, carrying the class's eight neighborhoods, seven pools and Category 6 waterpark with its own new-to-the-fleet entertainment lineup.
exp.who_for: Built as 'the ultimate family vacation' for multi-generational families and thrill-seekers — the family-focused Surfside neighborhood and Splashaway Bay for little ones, Category 6 and Crown's Edge for daredevils, and adults-only Hideaway and Cove pools for grown-ups.

dining.name: Surfside Eatery
dining.desc: Family buffet in the Surfside neighborhood.

dining.name: Park Cafe
dining.desc: Casual breakfast and lunch counter in Central Park.

dining.name: AquaDome Market
dining.desc: Food hall with made-to-order stands including sandwiches and crepes.

dining.name: Basecamp
dining.desc: Complimentary casual eatery in Thrill Island.

dining.name: Pearl Cafe
dining.desc: Coffee and toasted sandwiches on the Royal Promenade.

dining.name: Lincoln Park Supper Club
dining.desc: 1930s-inspired multi-course dinner-and-show with live jazz — new to Star of the Seas.

dining.name: Izumi in the Park
dining.desc: Casual walk-up sushi in Central Park.

dining.name: Desserted
dining.desc: Desserts and shakes in Thrill Island.

dining.name: Playmakers Sports Bar & Arcade
dining.desc: Sports bar with pub fare and arcade.

activities.name: Category 6
activities.desc: The largest waterpark at sea, with six record-breaking waterslides.

activities.name: Crown's Edge
activities.desc: Skywalk, ropes course and thrill ride combined, 154 feet above the ocean.

activities.name: Royal Bay Pool
activities.desc: The largest pool at sea, with in-water loungers and a live band, in Chill Island.

activities.name: The Cove Pool
activities.desc: Pool with a disappearing edge and in-water loungers.

activities.name: Ice Skating
activities.desc: Open sessions at the Absolute Zero ice arena.

entertainment.name: Back to the Future: The Musical
entertainment.desc: Full Broadway production staged in the main theater.

entertainment.name: Absolute Zero
entertainment.desc: Ice-skating spectacular arena with high-tech productions.

entertainment.name: AquaTheater show
entertainment.desc: AquaDome production with a 55-foot waterfall and high divers.

entertainment.name: H2Whoa!
entertainment.desc: High-tech water performance.

entertainment.name: Lou's Jazz 'n Blues
entertainment.desc: Live jazz and blues ensemble venue.

entertainment.name: Dueling Pianos
entertainment.desc: Interactive sing-along piano bar.

entertainment.name: Music Hall
entertainment.desc: Multi-deck live-music and nightlife venue.

entertainment.name: Boleros
entertainment.desc: Latin-themed dance lounge.

entertainment.name: Comedy Club
entertainment.desc: Stand-up comedy venue.

entertainment.name: Spotlight Karaoke
entertainment.desc: Karaoke bar.

neighbourhoods.name: Thrill Island
neighbourhoods.desc: Adventure neighborhood with Category 6 (the largest waterpark at sea), Crown's Edge 154 feet above the ocean, FlowRider and Lost Dunes mini-golf.

neighbourhoods.name: Chill Island
neighbourhoods.desc: Three-deck pool neighborhood home to four of the ship's seven pools, including Royal Bay, the largest pool at sea, and the Swim & Tonic swim-up bar.

neighbourhoods.name: Surfside
neighbourhoods.desc: Family-focused neighborhood with a pool, carousel and family dining.

neighbourhoods.name: Central Park
neighbourhoods.desc: Open-air garden neighborhood with live music and al-fresco dining.

neighbourhoods.name: The Hideaway
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only retreat built around a suspended infinity pool.

neighbourhoods.name: Royal Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: Two-story social hub with floor-to-ceiling ocean views, The Pearl kinetic art sculpture, and 15-plus restaurants, bars and lounges.

neighbourhoods.name: Suite Neighborhood
neighbourhoods.desc: Exclusive enclave for suite guests with private amenities including The Grove and Coastal Kitchen.

neighbourhoods.name: AquaDome
neighbourhoods.desc: Glass-and-steel crown of the ship housing the AquaTheater and AquaDome Market food hall.

kids_family.name: Surfside
kids_family.desc: Family neighborhood with pool, carousel and kid-friendly eateries.

kids_family.name: Splashaway Bay
kids_family.desc: Kids' aquapark with slides and sprayers.

kids_family.name: Adventure Ocean
kids_family.desc: Kids club with interactive games and age-based programming.

kids_family.name: Social020
kids_family.desc: Teens-only lounge with gaming, karaoke and movies.

exp.deploy_note: Caribbean, ex-Orlando (Port Canaveral), year-round since debut Aug 2025; 7-night alternating Eastern & Western Caribbean, every sailing calling at Perfect Day at CocoCay.

ships.name: Legend of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage July 4, 2026. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The third Icon Class ship, entering service in summer 2026 as the first of the class to sail Europe, carrying the same eight neighborhoods, seven pools and Category 6 — the largest waterpark at sea.
exp.who_for: Every type of family and vacationer across eight neighborhoods: families get the stay-all-day Surfside neighborhood, Splashaway Bay and a carousel; thrill-seekers get Category 6 and Crown's Edge; and adults get the adults-only Hideaway with its infinity-style pool.

dining.name: AquaDome Market
dining.desc: Food hall with five made-to-order stands.

activities.name: Category 6
activities.desc: The largest waterpark at sea, featuring Storm Surge and multiple record-breaking slides.

activities.name: Crown's Edge
activities.desc: Part skywalk, part ropes course and part thrill ride, 154 feet above the ocean.

activities.name: Ice skating
activities.desc: Open skating at Absolute Zero, the largest ice-skating arena at sea.

activities.name: Carousel
activities.desc: Full-size carousel in the Surfside family neighborhood.

activities.name: Royal Bay Pool
activities.desc: The largest pool at sea, part of seven pools and ten whirlpools.

activities.name: The Hideaway
activities.desc: Adults-only retreat with an infinity-style pool.

entertainment.name: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
entertainment.desc: Roald Dahl's tale staged as a full theatrical production.

entertainment.name: America's Got Talent LIVE on Legend of the Seas
entertainment.desc: Live variety production based on the TV franchise.

entertainment.name: AquaTheater show
entertainment.desc: High-diving and aerial production in the AquaDome.

entertainment.name: Ice skating show
entertainment.desc: Production show at Absolute Zero, the largest ice-skating arena at sea.

entertainment.name: Live music venues
entertainment.desc: Eight live-music venues across the ship.

entertainment.name: Themed parties
entertainment.desc: 10-plus party locations with trivia, game shows and themed nights.

neighbourhoods.name: Thrill Island
neighbourhoods.desc: Adventure neighborhood home to Category 6, the largest waterpark at sea.

neighbourhoods.name: Chill Island
neighbourhoods.desc: Resort-style pool deck with multiple of the ship's seven pools, including Royal Bay, the largest pool at sea.

neighbourhoods.name: Surfside
neighbourhoods.desc: Family-focused stay-all-day neighborhood with a carousel and family dining.

neighbourhoods.name: Central Park
neighbourhoods.desc: Open-air garden neighborhood with dining, live music and 30,000+ real plants.

neighbourhoods.name: The Hideaway
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only retreat with an infinity-style pool.

neighbourhoods.name: Royal Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: The vibrant heart of the ship for dining, shopping and nightlife.

neighbourhoods.name: Suite Neighborhood
neighbourhoods.desc: Exclusive spaces reserved for suite guests.

neighbourhoods.name: AquaDome
neighbourhoods.desc: Transformative crowning space with immersive entertainment and panoramic ocean views.

kids_family.name: Surfside
kids_family.desc: Stay-all-day family neighborhood with a carousel and family dining.

kids_family.name: Splashaway Bay
kids_family.desc: Aquapark for families with slides and sprayers.

kids_family.name: Kids and teen programs
kids_family.desc: Dedicated youth programming by age group.

exp.deploy_note: Debuts summer 2026: 7-night Western Mediterranean ex-Barcelona (with calls including Rome/Civitavecchia); then repositions to year-round Caribbean ex-Fort Lauderdale from Nov 2026 on 6-night Western & 8-night Southern Caribbean, calling at Perfect Day at CocoCay.

ships.name: Utopia of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage July 19, 2024. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The sixth Oasis-class ship and Royal Caribbean's first Oasis-class vessel powered by liquefied natural gas (LNG), purpose-built as 'the world's biggest weekend' for short getaways.
exp.who_for: Weekenders and first-timers who want a full mega-ship experience compressed into 3- and 4-night sailings, plus families drawn to Perfect Day at CocoCay and thrill-seekers after the Ultimate Abyss and Perfect Storm slides.

dining.name: Windjammer Marketplace
dining.desc: Complimentary buffet with a wide rotating spread.

dining.name: Solarium Bistro
dining.desc: Complimentary Mediterranean mezze-style plates in the adults-focused Solarium.

dining.name: El Loco Fresh
dining.desc: Complimentary Mexican fare with a build-your-own toppings bar.

dining.name: Royal Railway - Utopia Station
dining.desc: First-of-its-kind immersive dining car simulating a Wild West train journey with HD-screen 'windows' and a five-course menu.

dining.name: Giovanni's Italian Kitchen & Wine Bar
dining.desc: Italian dining with an alfresco Gio's Terrazza area.

dining.name: The Mason Jar Southern Restaurant & Bar
Izumi Hibachi & Sushi
dining.desc: Japanese teppanyaki and sushi with six hibachi tables.

dining.name: 150 Central Park
dining.desc: Upscale seasonal gourmet dining in the Central Park neighbourhood.

dining.name: Chef's Table
dining.desc: Intimate multi-course tasting menu with wine pairings.

dining.name: The Spare Tire
dining.desc: Poolside food truck serving taquito-style Tornados.

dining.name: Pesky Parrot
dining.desc: Caribbean-themed bar on the Royal Promenade with fruit-forward and frozen cocktails.

activities.name: The Ultimate Abyss
activities.desc: The longest dry slide at sea, 295 feet long, with zoom-booster rollers and transparent racing windows.

activities.name: The Perfect Storm waterslides
activities.desc: A trio of racing/tube waterslides on the top deck.

activities.name: FlowRider
activities.desc: Surf simulator using 30,000 gallons of water.

activities.name: Zip line
activities.desc: 82-foot-high zip line crossing over the Boardwalk.

activities.name: Ice skating rink
activities.desc: Full ice rink open for complimentary daytime skating.

entertainment.name: AquaTheater
entertainment.desc: Open-air aquatic theater with dual 30-foot dive towers and high-diving/acrobatic shows.

entertainment.name: Royal Theater
entertainment.desc: Main theater staging Broadway-style production shows.

entertainment.name: Studio B ice show
entertainment.desc: Ice-skating production in the Studio B rink.

entertainment.name: The Attic
entertainment.desc: Comedy club.

entertainment.name: Spotlight Karaoke
entertainment.desc: Dedicated karaoke venue with private and main-stage rooms.

entertainment.name: Boleros
entertainment.desc: Latin-music bar with live entertainment.

neighbourhoods.name: Central Park
neighbourhoods.desc: Open-air neighbourhood with real plantings and upscale dining.

neighbourhoods.name: Boardwalk
neighbourhoods.desc: Seaside-themed family neighbourhood anchored by the AquaTheater and carousel.

neighbourhoods.name: Royal Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: Indoor bustling promenade of bars, shops and cafes, home to Pesky Parrot.

neighbourhoods.name: Pool & Sports Zone
neighbourhoods.desc: Top-deck pools, waterslides, FlowRider and sports court.

neighbourhoods.name: Vitality Spa & Fitness Center
neighbourhoods.desc: Spa and fitness neighbourhood.

neighbourhoods.name: Entertainment Place
neighbourhoods.desc: Nightlife and shows district including the casino and karaoke.

neighbourhoods.name: Youth Zone
neighbourhoods.desc: Adventure Ocean kids and teen spaces.

exp.kids_family: Adventure Ocean youth programming plus Splashaway Bay aquapark, the carousel and Boardwalk; designed for short family getaways calling at Perfect Day at CocoCay.
exp.deploy_note: Home port Port Canaveral (Orlando), Florida; 3-night weekend and 4-night weekday sailings to Perfect Day at CocoCay and Nassau, The Bahamas.

ships.name: Wonder of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage March 4, 2022. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The fifth Oasis-class ship and, at delivery in early 2022, the world's largest cruise ship, spanning 18 decks and eight neighbourhoods.
exp.who_for: Multi-generational families wanting the most amenity-packed Oasis-class ship, including groups needing the two-level Ultimate Family Suite, plus travelers who want an eighth 'Suite Neighborhood' and week-long Caribbean itineraries.

dining.name: Solarium Bistro
dining.desc: Complimentary lighter-fare dining in the adults-only Solarium.

dining.name: The Mason Jar Southern Restaurant & Bar
150 Central Park
dining.desc: Upscale seasonal gourmet dining in Central Park.

dining.name: Johnny Rockets
dining.desc: Classic American diner burgers and shakes.

activities.name: The Ultimate Abyss
activities.desc: The tallest dry slide at sea.

activities.name: Zip line
activities.desc: Zip line running ten decks high over the Boardwalk.

activities.name: Splashaway Bay
activities.desc: Kids aquapark with slides, drench buckets and water cannons.

entertainment.name: AquaTheater
entertainment.desc: Open-air aquatic theater with high-diving and acrobatic shows.

entertainment.name: Studio B ice show
entertainment.desc: Ice-skating production in the Studio B rink.

entertainment.name: Music Hall
entertainment.desc: Two-level live-music venue with cover bands.

entertainment.name: Spotlight Karaoke
entertainment.desc: Dedicated karaoke venue.

entertainment.name: Vue Bar
entertainment.desc: Cantilevered bar with panoramic ocean views.

entertainment.name: Boleros
entertainment.desc: Latin-music bar.

neighbourhoods.name: Central Park
neighbourhoods.desc: Open-air garden neighbourhood with living plants and fine dining.

neighbourhoods.name: Boardwalk
neighbourhoods.desc: Family neighbourhood with the carousel and AquaTheater.

neighbourhoods.name: Royal Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: Indoor promenade of shops, bars and cafes.

neighbourhoods.name: Pool & Sports Zone
neighbourhoods.desc: Top-deck pools, waterslides, FlowRider and sports court.

neighbourhoods.name: Vitality Spa & Fitness Center
neighbourhoods.desc: Spa and fitness neighbourhood.

neighbourhoods.name: Entertainment Place
neighbourhoods.desc: Nightlife district with the casino, ice rink and Music Hall.

neighbourhoods.name: Youth Zone
neighbourhoods.desc: Adventure Ocean kids areas plus Wonder Playscape and Social100 teen space.

neighbourhoods.name: Suite Neighborhood
neighbourhoods.desc: Wonder's eighth neighbourhood, an exclusive suites-only area with private amenities.

exp.kids_family: Adventure Ocean programming, the open-air Wonder Playscape for younger kids, the Social100 teen hangout, Splashaway Bay aquapark, and the two-story Ultimate Family Suite with an in-room slide.
exp.deploy_note: Currently sailing Caribbean itineraries; Royal Caribbean's current ship page lists Miami, Florida as the home port (ship entered service at Port Canaveral in December 2022).

ships.name: Harmony of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage May 29, 2016. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The third Oasis-class ship, blending the class's iconic seven-neighbourhood layout with the debut of the Ultimate Abyss and the Perfect Storm waterslides across 16 guest decks.
exp.who_for: Families and active travelers who want the seven-neighbourhood Oasis experience with the tallest slide at sea, a nine-story zip line and the fleet's largest casino, on week-long Caribbean sailings.

dining.desc: Complimentary Mexican fare with a toppings bar.

exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's ship page lists Port Canaveral (Orlando), Florida as the home port.

ships.name: Symphony of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage April 7, 2018. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The fourth Oasis-class ship and, when delivered in 2018, the world's largest cruise ship, with seven neighbourhoods across 18 total decks (16 guest decks).
exp.who_for: Families and groups wanting a jam-packed big-ship experience with the Ultimate Family Suite, two FlowRiders, laser tag and Broadway-scale entertainment.

dining.desc: Complimentary lighter dining in the Solarium.

dining.name: 150 Central Park
dining.desc: Upscale seasonal gourmet dining in Central Park.

dining.name: Playmakers Sports Bar & Arcade
dining.desc: Sports bar and arcade with pub-style food.

activities.name: The Ultimate Abyss
activities.desc: Ten-story dry slide, the tallest slide at sea.

entertainment.name: AquaTheater
entertainment.desc: Open-air aquatic theater with acrobatic and high-diving shows on water and in the air.

exp.kids_family: Adventure Ocean youth programming, Splashaway Bay aquapark, laser tag, and the multi-level Ultimate Family Suite designed for larger families.
exp.deploy_note: Western Caribbean, ex-Galveston, Texas (Royal Caribbean's zero-energy terminal), 2026-27 season; 6- to 8-night itineraries including Costa Maya, Roatan, Nassau and Perfect Day at CocoCay, per Royal Caribbean press center.

ships.name: Allure of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage December 5, 2010. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The second Oasis-class ship, recently given a M-plus amplification, keeping the seven-neighbourhood layout while adding the Ultimate Abyss, Perfect Storm slides and new dining.
exp.who_for: Couples and families after one of the most awarded ships at sea, now newly amplified, with the tallest slide, a nine-story zip line and Broadway's Mamma Mia! on Caribbean sailings from Fort Lauderdale.

dining.name: The Mason Jar Southern Restaurant & Bar

activities.name: The Perfect Storm
activities.desc: Trio of waterslides (Typhoon, Cyclone and Supercell).

activities.name: Zip line
activities.desc: Zip line running nine stories above the Boardwalk.

activities.name: Splashaway Bay
activities.desc: Kids aquapark with twin drench buckets, three waterslides, fountains, pools, water cannons and a tots' Splash Pad.

entertainment.name: AquaTheater

ships.name: Oasis of the Seas

exp.overview: The original Oasis-class ship that pioneered the seven-neighbourhood concept when it debuted in 2009, since amplified with the Ultimate Abyss, Portside BBQ and a reimagined Caribbean pool deck.

ships.name: Odyssey of the Seas

exp.overview: The second and final Quantum Ultra-class ship, blending the class's engineering spectacle with the largest SeaPlex in the fleet and a glass North Star observation capsule.

ships.name: Brilliance of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage July 15, 2002. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: A Radiance-class ship defined by walls of glass, ocean-view glass elevators and a whirlpool-lined Solarium, offering panoramic sailing on a mid-size vessel.
exp.who_for: Best for couples and travelers who prize light-filled interiors, sea views and destination cruising over big-ship thrill rides.

dining.name: Chops Grille
dining.desc: Signature steakhouse serving steaks and Maine lobster.

dining.name: Izumi
dining.desc: Japanese cuisine and sushi from the Far East.

activities.name: Rock Climbing Wall
activities.desc: Climbing wall rising about 40 feet above deck.

entertainment.name: Main Theater
entertainment.desc: Broadway-inspired theatrical productions and live music.

neighbourhoods.name: Glass elevators
neighbourhoods.desc: Ocean-view glass elevators and expansive windows, a Radiance-class signature.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only glass-topped pool retreat.

exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page references Mediterranean itineraries (Athens, Italy, Croatia) and Bahamas sailings from Fort Lauderdale with a Royal Beach Club Paradise Island call. Confirm the current-season home port on the fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Jewel of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage May 8, 2004. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: A Radiance-class ship wrapped in glass with an indoor-outdoor adults-only Solarium and poolside movies under the stars, blending sea views with a relaxed mid-size feel.
exp.who_for: Best for couples and families who want light-filled, view-rich spaces and easygoing sea days rather than large-ship attractions.

dining.name: Vintages
dining.desc: Wine bar for tastings and by-the-glass pours.

activities.name: Poolside Movies
activities.desc: Films screened poolside under the stars.

entertainment.name: Royal Theater
entertainment.desc: Broadway-style production shows including 'City of Dreams.'

entertainment.name: Casino Royale
entertainment.desc: Onboard casino for table games and slots.

neighbourhoods.name: Glass-walled interiors
neighbourhoods.desc: Signature Radiance-class glass and ocean views throughout.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only pool retreat.

exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page references sailings from Fort Lauderdale and Tampa to the Bahamas (Perfect Day at CocoCay) and the Caribbean. Confirm current-season home port on the fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Radiance of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage April 7, 2001. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The lead ship of Royal Caribbean's Radiance class, wrapped in acres of glass with ocean-facing windows and glass elevators, and crowned by an adults-only Solarium pool beneath a retractable glass roof.
exp.who_for: Best for couples and scenery-seekers who want expansive ocean views and a calmer, mid-size ship built for destination-rich sailings rather than a floating theme park.

dining.name: Main Dining Room
dining.desc: Two-level complimentary main dining room, the ship's signature evening venue.

dining.name: Windjammer Marketplace
dining.desc: Complimentary casual buffet with global stations for breakfast, lunch and dinner.

dining.name: Park Cafe
dining.desc: Complimentary grab-and-go cafe for salads, sandwiches and snacks.

dining.name: Boardwalk Dog House
dining.desc: Complimentary quick-service spot for hot dogs and sausages.

dining.name: Chops Grille
dining.desc: Signature steakhouse serving premium cuts and seafood.

dining.name: Giovanni's Table
dining.desc: Rustic Italian kitchen with family-style dishes.

activities.name: Rock Climbing Wall
activities.desc: Signature climbing wall overlooking the sea.

activities.name: Adults-only Solarium
activities.desc: Indoor-outdoor adult retreat under a retractable glass roof with a bridge walkway.

entertainment.name: Main Theater
entertainment.desc: Original Royal Caribbean production shows and live performances.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Glass-roofed adults-only pool sanctuary, a Radiance-class hallmark.

neighbourhoods.name: Ocean-view glass elevators
neighbourhoods.desc: Glass-walled elevators and sweeping windows that flood the ship with light and sea views.

exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page lists Radiance of the Seas sailing 4-5 and 7-night Western Caribbean voyages from Tampa, Florida, calling at Cozumel and Costa Maya, Mexico. Home-port/season assignments rotate, so verify against the current fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Serenade of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage August 25, 2003. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: A Radiance-class ship spanning acres of glass with panoramic sea views, an adults-only Solarium and a nine-hole mini-golf course, purpose-built for scenic itineraries.
exp.who_for: Best for couples, families and view-lovers drawn to glass-walled spaces, self-leveling billiards, mini golf and warm-weather or Alaska scenery on a comfortable mid-size ship.

dining.name: Main Dining Room
dining.desc: Complimentary main dining room with original productions of the ship's core menu.

dining.name: Chef's Table
dining.desc: Intimate multi-course tasting experience with wine pairings.

activities.name: Mini Golf
activities.desc: Nine-hole miniature golf course on deck.

activities.name: Self-leveling Billiards
activities.desc: Gyroscopic pool tables that stay level as the ship moves.

activities.name: Adults-only Solarium
activities.desc: Adult pool retreat with sun lounging under the glass.

entertainment.name: Main Theater
entertainment.desc: Showstopping original Royal Caribbean productions.

entertainment.name: Schooner Bar
entertainment.desc: Nautical piano bar with singalongs.

entertainment.name: Boleros
entertainment.desc: Latin lounge for mojitos and salsa dancing.

neighbourhoods.name: Acres of glass
neighbourhoods.desc: Panoramic windows and glass elevators delivering ocean views across the ship.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only glass-topped pool sanctuary.

neighbourhoods.name: Casino Royale
neighbourhoods.desc: Onboard casino for table games and slots.

exp.kids_family: Adventure Ocean, Royal Caribbean's award-winning youth program, is offered on board.
exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page lists Serenade of the Seas sailing Mexican Riviera voyages from San Diego and Alaska voyages from Vancouver. Confirm current-season dates on the fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Enchantment of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage July 13, 1997. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: A Vision-class ship lengthened in 2005, notable for a Jump Zone bungee-trampoline deck, suspended bridges and an expanded pool deck atop the class's signature glass-and-Solarium design.
exp.who_for: Best for active families and value-minded cruisers who want a smaller, quainter ship with standout deck attractions like bungee trampolines and suspension bridges.

dining.name: Park Cafe
dining.desc: Complimentary deli known for its Kummelweck roast beef sandwich.

dining.name: Chops Grille
dining.desc: Steakhouse featuring prime cuts and seafood.

activities.name: Jump Zone Bungee Trampolines
activities.desc: Four bungee trampolines on Deck 10 where guests harness in for two-minute jump sessions.

activities.name: Suspension Bridges
activities.desc: Elevated bridges spanning the expanded pool deck.

activities.name: Solarium
activities.desc: Adults-only Solarium with a retractable glass roof (reserved for guests 16 and older).

entertainment.name: Stage to Screen
entertainment.desc: Original Broadway-style production show.

entertainment.name: Can't Stop the Rock
entertainment.desc: Rock-and-roll themed production show.

entertainment.name: Boleros
entertainment.desc: Latin lounge with a live six-piece band and salsa dancing.

entertainment.name: Casino
entertainment.desc: Onboard casino for adults.

neighbourhoods.name: Expanded Pool Deck
neighbourhoods.desc: Enlarged pool deck added in the 2005 lengthening, with the suspended bridges and Jump Zone.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Glass-roofed adults-only pool retreat.

exp.kids_family: Kid-focused spaces include an arcade with basketball and games; the Solarium is reserved for guests 16 and older.
exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page lists Enchantment of the Seas sailing Western Caribbean voyages from Tampa, Florida, calling at Cozumel, Costa Maya, Belize City, Roatan and Perfect Day at CocoCay. Confirm current-season dates on the fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Grandeur of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage December 14, 1996. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: A Vision-class ship built around a multi-deck Centrum atrium and glass-walled interiors, offering a more intimate, classic Royal Caribbean experience.
exp.who_for: Best for couples and families who prefer a smaller, easygoing ship with a lively pool deck and traditional cruise amenities over big-ship attractions.

dining.name: Windjammer
dining.desc: Complimentary casual buffet with global dishes.

activities.name: Pool Deck
activities.desc: Main pool area with DJ entertainment and whirlpools.

activities.name: Arcade
activities.desc: Games arcade for classic and contemporary play.

entertainment.name: Main Theater
entertainment.desc: Broadway-style production shows such as 'Rhythm & Rhyme.'

entertainment.name: Poolside Movies
entertainment.desc: Outdoor film screenings on deck.

entertainment.name: Casino
entertainment.desc: Vegas-style casino gaming.

neighbourhoods.name: Centrum
neighbourhoods.desc: Multi-deck glass-walled atrium at the heart of the ship, a Vision-class signature.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only pool retreat with the Solarium Bar.

exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page references Eastern, Western and Southern Caribbean itineraries from ports including Miami and Fort Lauderdale, with calls at Perfect Day at CocoCay, Curacao and Aruba. Confirm current-season home port on the fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Rhapsody of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage May 19, 1997. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: A Vision-class ship centered on a glass-walled Centrum atrium, delivering a compact, classic Royal Caribbean cruise with abundant sea views.
exp.who_for: Best for couples and travelers who want an intimate, destination-focused ship with traditional dining and entertainment rather than large-ship thrill features.

dining.name: Park Cafe
dining.desc: Complimentary cafe home to the fleet-famous Kummelweck sandwich.

activities.name: Rock Climbing Wall
activities.desc: Climbing wall rising about 40 feet above deck.

activities.name: Pool Deck
activities.desc: Main and portside pools with sun lounging.

entertainment.name: Main Theater
entertainment.desc: Broadway-style production shows.

entertainment.name: Piano Man
entertainment.desc: Live musical show celebrating piano hits.

entertainment.name: Casino Royale
entertainment.desc: Onboard casino for table games and slots.

neighbourhoods.name: Centrum
neighbourhoods.desc: Glass-walled multi-deck atrium at the ship's core, a Vision-class hallmark.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only pool retreat.

exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official ship page references Southern Caribbean sailings from San Juan, Puerto Rico, and Mediterranean itineraries (Greece, including the new Royal Beach Club Santorini). Confirm current-season home port on the fact sheet before publishing.

ships.name: Vision of the Seas
ships.notes: Guests = double occupancy (per official fact sheet). Maiden voyage May 2, 1998. Max occupancy not published on official fact sheet.

exp.overview: The namesake and last-built ship of Royal Caribbean's Vision class, wrapped in glass around a seven-deck Centrum atrium with a Solarium under a sliding glass roof and the signature Viking Crown Lounge perched high above.
exp.who_for: Best for couples and travelers seeking a smaller, more intimate glass-walled ship with classic amenities and no big-ship thrill features like a FlowRider or waterslides.

dining.name: Chef's Table
dining.desc: Fine-dining tasting menu hosted by the Chef de Cuisine.

dining.name: Izumi
dining.desc: Far East cuisine specializing in sushi.

activities.name: Solarium
activities.desc: Adults-only pool area beneath a sliding glass roof.

entertainment.name: Main Theater
entertainment.desc: Broadway-style production shows including the 'Boogie Wonderland' revue.

entertainment.name: R Bar
entertainment.desc: Retro-styled classic cocktail lounge.

neighbourhoods.name: Centrum
neighbourhoods.desc: Seven-deck glass-walled atrium at the heart of the ship.

neighbourhoods.name: Viking Crown Lounge
neighbourhoods.desc: Signature glass-walled lounge perched high on the ship with sweeping views.

neighbourhoods.name: Solarium
neighbourhoods.desc: Adults-only pool sanctuary under a sliding glass roof.

exp.kids_family: Royal Caribbean's official ship page cites family-friendly programming on board; specific youth-program details need verification against the fact sheet.
exp.deploy_note: Royal Caribbean's official pages reference Vision of the Seas sailing from Baltimore to Bermuda, the Bahamas, Canada/New England and the Southern Caribbean. Confirm current-season home port on the fact sheet before publishing.

needs_manual: ALL ships: maximum (non-double) occupancy — official fact sheets publish only double-occupancy guest counts.
Hero of the Seas — EXCLUDED: official fact sheet lists maiden voyage August 11, 2027 (future / not yet in service).
flag: 30 in-service '...of the Seas' ships. Roster confirmed against official royalcaribbean.com/cruise-ships fleet page AND the official press-center LoadShips API (which also supplied each ship's class grouping). Per-ship year/tonnage/guests/features read directly from official Royal Caribbean 'Fast Facts' fact-sheet images (royalcaribbeanmedia.com, first-party, one per ship). 'year' is derived from the printed MAIDEN VOYAGE date. All guest figures are DOUBLE OCCUPANCY; no official page publishes max occupancy. Hero of the Seas (Icon class) excluded as not-yet-in-service (maiden voyage Aug 11, 2027). No third-party sources used. No prices present on fact sheets.
